message:
test(fuzz): add OpenFs journal-replay fuzz harness + seeded corpus

Lands a new libfuzzer target that exercises the full ext4
OpenFs::open journal-replay dispatch surface for FrankenFS in a
single fuzz_target, plus a tiny seeded corpus so the harness finds
each of the four primary seed classes on a fresh worker.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs (+863):
- `fuzz_target!` clamps inputs to MAX_INPUT_BYTES (1024) and
  routes bytes through a ByteCursor into a SeedCase selector.
  Each seed class constructs a fully-synthesized ext4 image (256
  KiB, 4 KiB blocks, INODE_SIZE = 256) with the appropriate
  superblock fields flipped (`EXT4_STATE_OFFSET`,
  `EXT4_COMPAT_OFFSET`, `EXT4_JOURNAL_UUID_OFFSET`,
  `EXT4_JOURNAL_INUM_OFFSET`, `EXT4_JOURNAL_DEV_OFFSET`,
  `EXT4_LAST_ORPHAN_OFFSET`) via helpers so the OpenFs::open path
  reaches:
    - internal-journal apply
    - fast-commit tail replay (`Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence` via
      `build_fc_tag`, `build_fc_inode_update_transaction`,
      `pad_fc_stream_to_block_boundary`)
    - external-journal required recovery
      (`build_external_journal_image` with dirty_recovery flag)
    - WAL fail-fast replay
      (`build_wal_bytes` driving ffs_mvcc wal_replay)
- Classifies the open outcome into a compact `OutcomeClass`
  (Open { block_size, dirty_recovery, journal, fast_commit, wal,
  external_journal } | Err { errno, detail }) and runs
  `classify_open(&case, &options)` twice per input, asserting the
  two classifications are identical -- this is the determinism
  invariant for the OpenFs replay dispatch: equivalent inputs
  must observe equivalent journal / fast-commit / WAL replay
  outcomes.
- For seed WAL harnesses asserts `wal.records_discarded <= 2` so
  the fuzzer doesn't silently accept a harness that starts
  dropping records; crossing that bound is a signal to triage.
- Helpers include `build_ext4_image_with_inode`,
  `write_jbd2_header`, `write_jbd2_superblock_v2`,
  `set_test_journal_uuid`, `mutate_byte`, `xor_byte`,
  `journal_inode_offset`, and the four primary image builders
  `build_ext4_image_with_internal_journal`,
  `build_ext4_image_with_fast_commit_evidence`,
  `build_ext4_image_with_external_journal`, and
  `build_wal_bytes`. All stay inside the fuzz crate.

fuzz/Cargo.toml (+5):
- Register the new target as a `[[bin]]` between
  `fuzz_openfs_recovery` and `fuzz_ioctl_dispatch`, with
  `path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs"` and
  `doc = false`, matching the existing pattern so `cargo fuzz run
  fuzz_openfs_journal_replay` picks it up.

fuzz/corpus/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay/ (+4 seeds):
- `seed_internal_apply` (29 B) -- selector byte 0 steers into
  `build_ext4_image_with_internal_journal`.
- `seed_fast_commit_tail` (23 B) -- selector byte 1 steers into
  the fast-commit replay path.
- `seed_external_required` (33 B) -- selector byte 3 steers into
  external-journal dirty recovery.
- `seed_wal_failfast` (19 B) -- selector byte 4 steers into the
  ffs_mvcc WAL fail-fast path.

No production OpenFs / journal / WAL code change -- this is a
differential / determinism fuzz harness for the journal-replay
dispatch, mirroring the pattern of the existing fuzz_openfs_*
targets in this crate.

files.patch:
@@ -134,6 +134,11 @@ name = "fuzz_openfs_recovery"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_recovery.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_openfs_journal_replay"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_ioctl_dispatch"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch.rs"
